notes: Shanghai isolate series; MRBP reported as increasing from 36.4% in 2016 to 97.2% in 2022.
Shanghai isolate series; regional high-resistance anchor before the 2024 multicenter outbreak study.
Five sentinel hospitals in China during 2024; 393/394 isolates displayed high-level azithromycin resistance.
Nationwide Australian tNGS study of 2024-positive respiratory specimens estimated macrolide resistance at 8/188 (4.3%).
ESR national reference laboratory series from 1991-1995: 88 strains tested, with erythromycin MICs 0.12-0.5 mg/L. A later meta-analysis tabulates this study as 0/88 resistant isolates.
Health New Zealand notes MRBP has been detected by PHF Science and is being assessed; no public national fraction was located, so use a low detected/imported anchor pending surveillance.
UK enhanced-surveillance isolates collected from 2001-2009: all 583 isolates were fully susceptible to erythromycin, clarithromycin, and azithromycin.
UKHSA national reference laboratory poster: 661 B. pertussis isolates received from June 2023 to November 2024, with 2 predicted and phenotypically confirmed macrolide-resistant isolates.
Intermountain West pediatric isolates recovered from January 1985 to June 1997; 1/47 was erythromycin resistant (MIC 32 ug/mL).

CDC surveillance abstract: 1208 B. pertussis isolates collected from 2011-2015 across 7 enhanced-surveillance states, 2 outbreak states, and 6 sporadic-state settings were susceptible to erythromycin and azithromycin; 54 DNA NPS extracts had no A2047G mutation.
Osaka case series, August 2024-January 2025; 7/8 analyzed B. pertussis strains were macrolide resistant.
Japanese children at six clinics, March-August 2025; 43/52 sequenced B. pertussis isolates carried the A2047G mutation associated with macrolide resistance.
No public Swedish MRBP prevalence estimate was located in the accessible national surveillance material, so keep a conservative low anchor pending numeric reporting.

PAHO reported antibiotic-resistant pertussis cases detected in Brazil since 2024, but no public national resistant fraction or denominator was located, so keep a conservative low detected anchor pending numeric reporting.

No public Thailand-specific MRBP prevalence estimate was located in accessible surveillance material; CDC notes increasing MRBP globally, so keep a conservative low imported anchor pending numeric reporting.
